intro: Pouteria crassipedicellata, also known as the "Brazilian cherry" or "Açafrão", is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The bark and leaves of this plant have been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, inflammation, and gastrointestinal problems, due to its potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Pouteria crassipedicellata include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
